```format
Status: Reviewed `docs/handbook/methodology/lean-ai-accelerated-methodology.md` end-to-end; cross-checked operational claims against repo: `infra/ci/pr.yml`, `infra/ci/promote.yml`, `packages/config/flags.ts`, `infra/otel/instrumentation.ts`, `apps/api/src/log.ts`.

Verdict: The document is high quality but has several CI/gating inconsistencies and a few ambiguities; no hard conflicts. Issues found: Major and Minor.

1) CI claims Python support that isn’t implemented
   - Where: `docs/handbook/methodology/lean-ai-accelerated-methodology.md` §7) CI/CD Quality Gates | lines 119–121
   - Text: "The pipeline supports TypeScript and Python."
   - Issue: inconsistency — Current CI (`infra/ci/pr.yml`) has no Python steps (no ruff/black/mypy).
   - Severity: Major
   - Why it matters: Readers will expect Python lint/type/test gates that don’t run, causing confusion and false expectations.
   - Fix: Reword to "The pipeline supports TypeScript. Python gates are optional and can be added per-package when Python is introduced."

2) “Required” contract tests (Pact, Schemathesis) not present in CI
   - Where: `docs/handbook/methodology/lean-ai-accelerated-methodology.md` §7) CI/CD Quality Gates → Checklist | lines 144–149
   - Text: "contract tests (Pact), OpenAPI schema checks (Schemathesis)"
   - Issue: inconsistency — CI runs `oasdiff` but not Pact or Schemathesis.
   - Severity: Major
   - Why it matters: The “required to merge” checklist promises gates that aren’t enforced.
   - Fix: Change “required” to “recommended (adopt incrementally)” or add jobs for Pact/Schemathesis; keep `oasdiff` as baseline.

3) Playwright preview smoke tests listed as required but absent
   - Where: `docs/handbook/methodology/lean-ai-accelerated-methodology.md` §7) CI/CD Quality Gates | lines 130–131, 144–145
   - Text: "E2E Smoke: Playwright vs Preview URL"
   - Issue: inconsistency — CI does not run Playwright against the preview URL.
   - Severity: Major
   - Why it matters: Missing promised smoke checks reduces confidence in preview quality gates.
   - Fix: Reword to “recommended” or add a Playwright job that targets the Vercel preview URL before merge.

4) License scan tool mismatch (license-checker vs what CI runs)
   - Where: `docs/handbook/methodology/lean-ai-accelerated-methodology.md` §5) Spec‑First + BMAD | lines 101–103; §7) Checklist | lines 146–149
   - Text: "Run license checks ... (`license-checker`, `pip-licenses`)" and "Dependencies ... license scan"
   - Issue: inconsistency — CI uses GitHub Dependency Review and SBOM; no `license-checker` step.
   - Severity: Major
   - Why it matters: Teams may wait for a license gate that never runs.
   - Fix: Note “License gate via GitHub Dependency Review + SBOM (Syft) today; `license-checker` optional.”

5) Secrets scanning tool mismatch (Gitleaks vs TruffleHog)
   - Where: `docs/handbook/methodology/lean-ai-accelerated-methodology.md` §7) Checklist | lines 147–148
   - Text: "Secret scan: GitHub ... + Gitleaks"
   - Issue: inconsistency — CI uses TruffleHog, not Gitleaks (GitHub native secret scanning is separate).
   - Severity: Major
   - Why it matters: Operational instructions don’t match the actual scanner in CI.
   - Fix: Replace “Gitleaks” with “TruffleHog (or Gitleaks — pick one)” and state the current choice is TruffleHog.

6) Bundle size budgets marked as enforced but no CI step
   - Where: `docs/handbook/methodology/lean-ai-accelerated-methodology.md` §7) Checklist | lines 149–151
   - Text: "enforce bundle size budgets (`size-limit`)."
   - Issue: inconsistency — No `size-limit` or equivalent step in `infra/ci/pr.yml`.
   - Severity: Major
   - Why it matters: Performance guardrail is claimed but not enforced, risking regressions.
   - Fix: Change to “recommended” or add a `size-limit` step; publish thresholds in repo.

7) “Required checks: all CI gates from §7” overstates what runs
   - Where: `docs/handbook/methodology/lean-ai-accelerated-methodology.md` §14) Tooling Map | lines 274–277
   - Text: "Required checks: all CI gates from §7."
   - Issue: inconsistency — CI implements a subset (typecheck/lint/test/build, CodeQL, Semgrep, Dependency Review, TruffleHog, SBOM, `oasdiff`, preview).
   - Severity: Major
   - Why it matters: Creates a gap between policy and enforcement.
   - Fix: Reword to “Required checks: the CI gates currently configured in `infra/ci/pr.yml`; adopt the rest incrementally.”

8) Flag provider integration point is vague for current apps
   - Where: `docs/handbook/methodology/lean-ai-accelerated-methodology.md` §6) Branching & Release Model | line 112; §12) Architecture | line 220
   - Text: "Call `setFlagProvider(vercelFlagsProvider)` during application startup ..."
   - Issue: ambiguity — It’s unclear where to register in this repo (Astro SSG app and Node API), and no provider impl is present.
   - Severity: Minor
   - Why it matters: Teams may not know where to wire the provider, leading to env-only behavior.
   - Fix: Specify: “Register in `apps/api/src/server.ts` (API) and in SSR entry when adding SSR surfaces; Astro SSG should inject flags at build or via Edge middleware.”

9) Astro SSG flags guidance could clarify client vs server usage
   - Where: `docs/handbook/methodology/lean-ai-accelerated-methodology.md` §12) Architecture | line 220
   - Text: "On Astro SSG/static pages ... do not rely on `process.env` in the browser."
   - Issue: ambiguity — Not explicit that `isFlagEnabled` should only be called server-side or via injected data.
   - Severity: Minor
   - Why it matters: Prevents accidental client-side env access or mismatches.
   - Fix: Add: “Use `isFlagEnabled` only on the server; for SSG, serialize flag values into the page at build-time or fetch via Edge/API.”

10) Load-test gating described but not implemented
    - Where: `docs/handbook/methodology/lean-ai-accelerated-methodology.md` §11) Performance & Scalability | lines 208–209
    - Text: "Gate merges if p95 exceeds budget by >10%."
    - Issue: inconsistency — No CI job performs load tests or enforces this gate.
    - Severity: Minor
    - Why it matters: Performance SLO claims aren’t enforced, risking regressions.
    - Fix: Reword to “recommended policy” or add a conditional load-test job targeting the Preview URL with pass/fail thresholds.

Summary: 0 Critical, 6 Major, 4 Minor. Themes: CI/gating language overstates current enforcement; tooling names diverge from what’s actually configured; flag provider wiring needs explicit per-app guidance. Minimal fixes are wording adjustments and clear adoption notes; optionally add CI steps to match the doc.
```

```plaintext
Proposal: Add a Python AI Gateway service (FastAPI + LiteLLM) under `apps/ai-service` for model-agnostic LLM/embeddings endpoints with OpenTelemetry and JSON logs. Integrate with the monorepo via a lightweight `package.json` for Turbo/PNPM orchestration, and extend CI to run Python gates conditionally. Provide a TypeScript adapter in `packages/adapters` to consume the gateway from apps.

\```plaintext
// Suggested repo additions (new files)
apps/
  ai-service/
    app/
      __init__.py
      main.py
      schemas.py
      otel.py
    tests/
      test_health.py
    pyproject.toml
    README.md
    package.json  # bridge to Turbo via PNPM scripts calling `uv`
\```

\```toml
# apps/ai-service/pyproject.toml
[project]
name = "ai-service"
version = "0.0.1"
description = "Python AI Gateway for LLM and embeddings"
requires-python = ">=3.11"
dependencies = [
  "fastapi>=0.115",
  "uvicorn[standard]>=0.30",
  "pydantic>=2.9",
  "structlog>=24.1",
  "httpx>=0.27",
  "litellm>=1.40.0",
  "opentelemetry-sdk>=1.26.0",
  "opentelemetry-instrumentation-fastapi>=0.47b0",
  "opentelemetry-exporter-otlp-proto-http>=1.26.0",
  "opentelemetry-instrumentation-logging>=0.47b0",
  "pip-licenses>=4.5.1"
]

[tool.uv]
# No special config needed; `uv` will lock/install via this file.

[tool.ruff]
line-length = 100
target-version = "py311"

[tool.black]
line-length = 100
target-version = ["py311"]

[tool.mypy]
python_version = "3.11"
strict = true
warn_unused_ignores = true
disallow_untyped_defs = true
warn_return_any = true

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
addopts = "-q"
asyncio_mode = "auto"
\```

\```python
# apps/ai-service/app/schemas.py
from __future__ import annotations

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional, List


class CompletionRequest(BaseModel):
    prompt: str = Field(..., description="User prompt")
    model: Optional[str] = Field(default=None, description="LLM model identifier")
    temperature: Optional[float] = Field(default=0.7, ge=0.0, le=2.0)
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = Field(default=256, ge=1)


class CompletionResponse(BaseModel):
    output: str
    model: str
    promptTokens: int
    completionTokens: int
    totalTokens: int


class EmbeddingRequest(BaseModel):
    input: str = Field(..., description="Text to embed")
    model: Optional[str] = Field(default=None, description="Embedding model identifier")


class EmbeddingResponse(BaseModel):
    embedding: List[float]
    model: str
\```

\```python
# apps/ai-service/app/otel.py
from __future__ import annotations

import os
from typing import Optional

from fastapi import FastAPI
from opentelemetry import trace
from opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.http.trace_exporter import OTLPSpanExporter
from opentelemetry.instrumentation.fastapi import FastAPIInstrumentor
from opentelemetry.instrumentation.logging import LoggingInstrumentation
from opentelemetry.sdk.resources import Resource
from opentelemetry.sdk.trace import TracerProvider
from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export import BatchSpanProcessor


def init_otel(app: FastAPI, service_name: str = "ai-service") -> Optional[callable]:
    """
    Initialize OpenTelemetry tracing and instrument FastAPI and logging.

    Returns an optional shutdown callable for graceful teardown.
    """
    otlp_endpoint = os.getenv("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT", "http://localhost:4318")
    resource = Resource.create({"service.name": service_name})

    provider = TracerProvider(resource=resource)
    trace.set_tracer_provider(provider)

    span_exporter = OTLPSpanExporter(endpoint=f"{otlp_endpoint}/v1/traces")
    provider.add_span_processor(BatchSpanProcessor(span_exporter))

    FastAPIInstrumentor.instrument_app(app)
    LoggingInstrumentation(set_logging_format=True)

    def _shutdown() -> None:
        provider.shutdown()

    return _shutdown
\```

\```python
# apps/ai-service/app/main.py
from __future__ import annotations

import os
import structlog
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
from litellm import acompletion, aembedding  # async LiteLLM APIs
from app.schemas import (
    CompletionRequest,
    CompletionResponse,
    EmbeddingRequest,
    EmbeddingResponse,
)
from app.otel import init_otel

logger = structlog.get_logger().bind(svc="ai-service")

app = FastAPI(title="AI Gateway", version="0.0.1")
_shutdown_otel = init_otel(app)


@app.get("/health")
async def health() -> dict[str, str]:
    return {"status": "ok"}


@app.post("/v1/llm/completions", response_model=CompletionResponse)
async def create_completion(req: CompletionRequest) -> CompletionResponse:
    """
    Minimal model-agnostic completion endpoint using LiteLLM.
    Provider API keys are read from env (e.g., OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).
    """
    model = req.model or os.getenv("LITELLM_MODEL", "gpt-4o-mini")
    try:
        resp = await acompletion(
            model=model,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": req.prompt}],
            temperature=req.temperature or 0.7,
            max_tokens=req.max_tokens or 256,
        )
        content = resp.choices[0].message["content"]
        usage = resp.usage or {}
        return CompletionResponse(
            output=content,
            model=resp.model or model,
            promptTokens=int(usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0)),
            completionTokens=int(usage.get("completion_tokens", 0)),
            totalTokens=int(usage.get("total_tokens", 0)),
        )
    except Exception as e:
        logger.exception("completion_error", err=str(e), model=model)
        raise HTTPException(status_code=502, detail="Upstream LLM error") from e


@app.post("/v1/embeddings", response_model=EmbeddingResponse)
async def create_embedding(req: EmbeddingRequest) -> EmbeddingResponse:
    """
    Vendor-agnostic embeddings via LiteLLM.
    """
    model = req.model or os.getenv("LITELLM_EMBED_MODEL", "text-embedding-3-small")
    try:
        resp = await aembedding(model=model, input=req.input)
        vec = resp.data[0]["embedding"]  # type: ignore[index]
        return EmbeddingResponse(embedding=vec, model=model)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.exception("embedding_error", err=str(e), model=model)
        raise HTTPException(status_code=502, detail="Upstream embeddings error") from e
\```

# apps/ai-service/tests/test_health.py
import pytest
from httpx import AsyncClient
from app.main import app


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_health_ok():
    async with AsyncClient(app=app, base_url="http://test") as ac:
        r = await ac.get("/health")
        assert r.status_code == 200
        assert r.json() == {"status": "ok"}

  "name": "@harmony/ai-service",
  "private": true,
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000",
    "build": "uv sync",
    "typecheck": "uv run mypy app",
    "lint": "uv run ruff check . && uv run black --check .",
    "format": "uv run black .",
    "test": "uv run pytest -q",
    "licenses": "uv run pip-licenses --format=json --with-urls --with-license-file --output-file reports/pip-licenses.json || true"
  }
}

# infra/ci/pr.yml (add a Python quality job and make preview depend on it)
# 1) Add this new job:
python:
  if: ${{ hashFiles('apps/ai-service/pyproject.toml') != '' }}
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      with: { python-version: "3.11" }
    - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v3
    - name: Install deps
      working-directory: apps/ai-service
      run: uv sync
    - name: Lint (ruff) + Format check (black)
      working-directory: apps/ai-service
      run: uv run ruff check . && uv run black --check .
    - name: Typecheck (mypy)
      working-directory: apps/ai-service
      run: uv run mypy app
    - name: Tests (pytest)
      working-directory: apps/ai-service
      run: uv run pytest -q
    - name: Python license scan
      working-directory: apps/ai-service
      run: uv run pip-licenses --format=json --output-file reports/pip-licenses.json || true

# 2) Update the preview job `needs` to wait on Python too:
# before:
#   needs: quality
# after:
#   needs: [quality, python]
\```

\```ts
// packages/adapters/src/ai-gateway.ts
// ESM module: Node 22+ has global fetch.
// Exposes a typed client for apps to call the Python AI Gateway.

export interface GenerateCompletionDTO {
  prompt: string;
  model?: string;
  temperature?: number;
  maxTokens?: number;
}

export interface CompletionResponseDTO {
  output: string;
  model: string;
  promptTokens: number;
  completionTokens: number;
  totalTokens: number;
}

export interface GenerateEmbeddingDTO {
  input: string;
  model?: string;
}

export interface EmbeddingResponseDTO {
  embedding: number[];
  model: string;
}

/**
 * Error class for AI Gateway failures.
 */
export class AIServiceError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string, public status?: number) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "AIServiceError";
  }
}

/**
 * Contract for the AI Gateway client.
 */
export interface AIGateway {
  generateCompletion(
    req: GenerateCompletionDTO,
    signal?: AbortSignal
  ): Promise<CompletionResponseDTO>;
  generateEmbedding(
    req: GenerateEmbeddingDTO,
    signal?: AbortSignal
  ): Promise<EmbeddingResponseDTO>;
}

const DEFAULT_AI_BASE_URL = process.env.AI_SERVICE_URL || "http://localhost:8000";

/**
 * Create a typed client to the Python AI Gateway.
 * Uses JSON over HTTP with simple retries handled by callers as needed.
 */
export default function createAIGateway(baseUrl: string = DEFAULT_AI_BASE_URL): AIGateway {
  async function handle<T>(res: Response): Promise<T> {
    if (!res.ok) {
      const text = await res.text().catch(() => "");
      throw new AIServiceError(text || `AI service error (${res.status})`, res.status);
    }
    return (await res.json()) as T;
  }

  return {
    async generateCompletion(req, signal) {
      const res = await fetch(new URL("/v1/llm/completions", baseUrl), {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify(req),
        signal
      });
      return handle<CompletionResponseDTO>(res);
    },

    async generateEmbedding(req, signal) {
      const res = await fetch(new URL("/v1/embeddings", baseUrl), {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify(req),
        signal
      });
      return handle<EmbeddingResponseDTO>(res);
    }
  };
}
\```

\```plaintext
Env & ops notes:
- Required env (Python service):
  - `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` (default `http://localhost:4318`)
  - Provider keys as needed by LiteLLM (e.g., `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`)
  - `LITELLM_MODEL`, `LITELLM_EMBED_MODEL` (defaults in code)
- Local dev:
  - Start AI service: `cd apps/ai-service && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000`
  - Call from TS via `createAIGateway()` with `AI_SERVICE_URL=http://localhost:8000`
- Security/limits (follow-ups):
  - Add auth (e.g., HMAC or service token) and rate limiting (SlowAPI) before exposing publicly.
  - Keep provider API keys only in the Python service env; do not forward to clients.
- Observability:
  - Python traces exported to the same OTLP endpoint as Node; HTTP spans correlate via W3C trace headers automatically when caller is instrumented.

Impact:
- Clean, decoupled AI boundary: TS apps call a stable HTTP contract; Python handles vendor complexity and evaluation.
- CI alignment: Python gates run only when `apps/ai-service` exists, matching the doc’s intent for conditional Python support.
\```
```

```plaintext
- What it is: A model‑agnostic AI Gateway (REST microservice) that standardizes access to LLMs and embeddings behind a single HTTP API.
- Purpose: Provide a uniform, vendor‑neutral contract for text completions and embeddings across providers (via LiteLLM), centralizing keys, quotas, and policies.
- Tech stack: Python 3.11, FastAPI, LiteLLM, structlog (JSON logs), OpenTelemetry (OTLP), httpx; exported traces align with the repo’s OTEL setup.
- Endpoints (JSON over HTTP):
  - POST /v1/llm/completions → text generation (prompt, model, temperature, max_tokens)
  - POST /v1/embeddings → dense vector embeddings (input, model)
- Consumption: TypeScript apps call it through a typed HTTP client (e.g., `createAIGateway`) instead of talking to providers directly.
- Characteristics: Stateless, provider‑agnostic, easy to extend (moderation, reranking, tool‑calling schema validation, caching, batch jobs).
- What it isn’t: Not a training/finetuning pipeline, not a vector store, not a long‑running job/queue worker (those can be added later if needed).
- Why this fits AI‑heavy apps: Centralizes observability, auth/rate‑limits, and cost controls; lets you swap/compose providers without changing app code.
```
